title: Reviewer-Proof Z-Score Statistics: 100-Null CIs, BH-FDR, Pruning Robustness, and Layer-Preserving Validation at 200-Graph Scale
summary: Demo notebook implementing a six-phase statistical pipeline for analyzing network motifs in Neuronpedia attribution graphs. Processes 3 graphs from diverse domains (antonym, arithmetic, code_completion) through: Phase A (3-node motif Z-scores with 50 null models), Phase B (4-node Z-scores on pruned graphs), Phase C (Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction), Phase D (pruning sensitivity sweep across 3 thresholds), Phase E (convergence diagnostics), and Phase F (layer-preserving null model validation with Wilcoxon comparison). Uses DAG-constrained edge-swap randomization. Full pipeline completes in ~8 seconds. Visualizes Z-score distributions, FDR impact, and degree-preserving vs layer-preserving null model comparison.
